meta_title: For Students: SaaS starter kit to Solve tutorial hell (Intermediate Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome tutorial hell. A Intermediate-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS starter kit to Solve tutorial hell
content.seo_description: Learn how to build a SaaS starter kit and overcome tutorial hell. An intermediate-level guide using Antigravity and Claude Code to launch your first app.
content.answer_block: Tutorial hell ends when you stop watching and start architecting. By utilizing a SaaS starter kit, you shift from passive consumption to active production. Using Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative refinement, you will transform abstract concepts into a deployable, high-value product for your portfolio.
content.intro:
You have spent countless hours watching polished tutorials, yet your screen remains filled with boilerplate code that never sees the light of production. The cycle of passive consumption is a silent career killer, leaving you with knowledge gaps that only real-world engineering can fill. It is time to break the loop.

This guide is your transition from student to software architect. We are bypassing the fluff to build a robust SaaS starter kit. By combining the rapid scaffolding of Antigravity with the autonomous coding power of Claude Code, you will stop building 'to-do' lists and start shipping functional, scalable SaaS infrastructure.

steps.title: Architecting the Core Schema
steps.description: Establish a robust database schema using PostgreSQL and Prisma. This foundation ensures your SaaS can handle user authentication and subscription states from day one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Prisma schema for a multi-tenant SaaS application, including User, Organization, and Subscription models. Ensure strict relational integrity and indexing for performance.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --tasks 'Initialize Prisma, setup PostgreSQL connection, and generate migration files'

steps.title: Implementing Secure Authentication
steps.description: Integrate Auth.js to handle secure session management. This is the gatekeeper of your application and a critical skill for any developer.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure Auth.js with GitHub and Email providers, ensuring secure session callbacks and user role assignment for SaaS access levels.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --tasks 'Install and configure Auth.js, create secure API routes, and test login flow'

steps.title: Building the Subscription Engine
steps.description: Connect Stripe to your starter kit. Handling payments is the difference between a prototype and a real SaaS product.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Stripe webhook handler to sync subscription status with our database. Include logic for 'active', 'past_due', and 'canceled' states.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --tasks 'Integrate Stripe SDK, write webhook endpoint, and create a subscription status check middleware'

steps.title: Developing the UI Layer
steps.description: Use Tailwind CSS and shadcn/ui to build a professional, responsive dashboard interface that users will trust.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a dashboard layout with a sidebar navigation, top-level analytics cards, and a data table for user management using Tailwind CSS.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --tasks 'Scaffold dashboard layout, implement sidebar component, and add responsive grid for analytics'

steps.title: Deploying the Infrastructure
steps.description: Automate your deployment pipeline. A SaaS app is useless if it is trapped on your local machine.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Vercel deployment configuration file that includes environment variable validation and build-time optimization scripts.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --tasks 'Verify build configuration, setup production environment variables, and trigger initial deployment'

pro_tips: Always build for multi-tenancy from day one; retrofitting it later is a nightmare.
Prioritize 'Type Safety' across your entire stack to catch bugs before runtime.
Use mock data factories to test edge cases in your UI before the backend is fully operational.
Keep your Stripe webhooks idempotent to prevent duplicate billing cycles.
Focus on 'Time to First Commit'—the faster you deploy, the more momentum you maintain.
content.next_steps: Once your starter kit is deployed, add a unique feature—like an AI-powered summary tool or a custom dashboard widget—to make the project uniquely yours for your portfolio.
content.conversion_block: You are now equipped with the architecture to move beyond theory and into professional production. Stop stalling and start shipping. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
